process_name: Baryonic b -> s l+l- decay Lambda_b0 -> Lambda l+l-

evidence: quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 lists only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 as modern phenomenology systems.
flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75 implements check_mu_to_e_gamma; it is a lepton dipole constraint, not b -> s l+l-.
Targeted code-only search for Lambda_b, Lambda mu, b->s, C9, C10, and semileptonic returned no B021 backend; only Lambda_IR false positives appeared.
commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd scripts tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd scripts tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n -e 'Lambda_b' -e 'Lambda.*mu' -e 'b.?->.?s' -e 'C9' -e 'C10' -e 'semileptonic' quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd solvers tests -g '!*.ipynb'

status_history.reason: initial PKA draft deposit for baryonic b -> s l+l- process
checker batch ca_w5a_beauty found traceability/schema issues
issues: CHK-1: TeX post-2008 CDF numerical claims (24 signal events, 5.8 sigma, 6.8 fb^-1, 1.96 TeV, and BR = (1.73 +/-0.42 +/-0.55)e-6) are recorded under supporting_measurements rather than pdg_or_equivalent.
CHK-1: B021 pdg_or_equivalent observables lack an explicit year field in the YAML block.
CHK-2: source_manifest.yaml entries use local_snapshot_path instead of the required snapshot_path field.

status_history.reason: Cycle-2 writer rework: added CA-flagged CDF value metadata under pdg_or_equivalent and explicit years for existing observable blocks.
checker batch ca_w5a_beauty_v2 found remaining traceability issue
issues: CHK-1: TeX states that LHCb used 3.0 fb^-1, but the 3.0 fb^-1 dataset number remains only under supporting_measurements and is not promoted to pdg_or_equivalent.

status_history.reason: Opus arbitration APPROVE-OVERRIDE per plan v1 :620-622: WA/CA cycle-3 cap reached. CA's CHK-1 reading is over-aggressive. The LHCb 3.0 fb^-1 dataset integrated luminosity is dataset metadata, not a measured experimental observable; it correctly lives in supporting_measurements with full provenance (snapshot_path, sha256_of_text_snapshot, source_url, access_date) and points to the same LHCb 2015 snapshot already promoted under pdg_or_equivalent.observables for dBR/dq^2 and angular observables. Per the L001 precedent (flavor_catalog/signoff/by_process/L001.md), pdg_or_equivalent is for measured observables; experimental dataset normalization/luminosity metadata belongs in supporting_measurements (or paper_era_reference for theoretical normalization scales). No TeX or sidecar edits required. See flavor_catalog/signoff/by_process/B021_B023.md.

status_history.notes: All measured and quoted prediction value claims verified against fetched source pages or local source snapshots.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\Lambda_b^0\to\Lambda\ell^+\ell^-\).
This is the baryonic exclusive \(b\to s\ell^+\ell^-\) channel.  The current
canonical measured mode is \(\Lambda_b^0\to\Lambda\mu^+\mu^-\); no comparable
PDG electron-mode average was found in this PKA pass.
sections.pdg_prose:
The sidecar is the numerical source of truth.  PDG Live's 2025
\(\Lambda_b^0\) listing gives
\[
  \mathcal B(\Lambda_b^0\to\Lambda\mu^+\mu^-)
  =(1.08\pm0.28)\times10^{-6},
\]
from the decay-mode table under source key
\texttt{PDG2025:LambdaBToLambdaMuMu}.  The LHCb angular analysis under
\texttt{LHCb2015:LambdaBToLambdaMuMu} reports, for
\(15<q^2<20\,{\rm GeV}^2/c^4\),
\[
  \frac{d\mathcal B}{dq^2}
  =(1.18^{+0.09}_{-0.08}\pm0.03\pm0.27)\times10^{-7}
  ({\rm GeV}^2/c^4)^{-1},
\]
and \(A_{\rm FB}^{\ell}=-0.05\pm0.09\pm0.03\),
\(A_{\rm FB}^{h}=-0.29\pm0.07\pm0.03\).
Local snapshots are in \texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/B021/}.
sections.relevance:
This mode probes \(\Delta B=1\) semileptonic FCNC operators rather than the
\(\Delta F=2\) four-quark basis implemented for neutral meson mixing.  Warped
flavor models can generate flavor-changing \(Z\) couplings, KK electroweak
exchange, and loop-induced dipoles feeding \(C_7\), \(C_9\), \(C_{10}\), and
primed semileptonic coefficients.  The baryon final state adds spin and
polarization information unavailable in \(B\to K^{(*)}\ell^+\ell^-\), so it is
a complementary diagnostic of chirality and hadronic systematics.
sections.post_2008:
The arXiv:0804.1954 RS-flavor baseline predates this channel as an observed
rare decay (\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}).  CDF reported
the first observation in 2011 with 24 signal events, 5.8 standard-deviation
significance, and
\(\mathcal B=(1.73\pm0.42\pm0.55)\times10^{-6}\)
(\texttt{CDF2011:LambdaBToLambdaMuMu}).  LHCb later used
\(3.0\,{\rm fb}^{-1}\) to publish the \(q^2\)-binned branching-fraction and
angular analysis quoted above
(\texttt{LHCb2015:LambdaBToLambdaMuMu}).  Lattice-QCD work by Detmold, Lin,
Meinel, and Wingate supplied post-2008 \(\Lambda_b\to\Lambda\) form factors,
so this baryonic mode should not be folded into the mesonic
\(B\to K^{(*)}\ell\ell\) treatment
(\texttt{DetmoldLinMeinelWingate2013:LambdaBToLambdaFormFactors}).
sections.validity:
This is a useful catalog constraint but not a single-number hard bound.  The
interpretation depends on \(q^2\) binning, charmonium vetoes, normalization to
\(\Lambda_b^0\to J/\psi\Lambda\), baryonic form factors, and nonlocal charm
effects.  Its most robust role is as a \(\Delta B=1\) semileptonic cross-check
with different hadronic systematics from mesonic rare \(B\) decays.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The modern phenomenology surface lists only
\(\epsilon_K\), \(K\), \(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D^0\) at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}.  The only live
lepton-flavor module is the \(\mu\to e\gamma\) dipole check at
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}.  Required plan greps and targeted
searches for \(\Lambda_b\), \(b\to s\), \(C_9\), \(C_{10}\), and semileptonic
rare decays found no B021 implementation.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  A production constraint would need a new \(\Delta B=1\)
semileptonic Hamiltonian, RS matching onto \(C_7,C_9,C_{10}\) and primed
operators, baryonic form factors, \(q^2\)-bin predictions, angular likelihoods,
and experimental covariance/normalization treatment.
